title: puya lanata
intro: Puya lanata is a plant species native to the Andean highlands of South America, belonging to the family Asphodelaceae and characterized by its distinctive, fuzzy, or lanate leaves and stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, thanks to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Puya lanata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Puya lanata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and is also used in some herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
